name: Dyad
tagline: Local, open-source AI app builder that runs on your own machine
seoTitle: Dyad: Local Open-Source AI App Builder
metaDescription: Dyad is a free, local, open-source AI app builder for Mac and Windows. Bring your own AI keys, build apps privately on your machine, with no sign-up needed.
overview: Dyad is a local, open-source AI app builder. It runs right on your own computer, so your work stays fast, private, and fully under your control. It aims to do the same job as cloud tools like Lovable, v0, or Bolt, but without sending everything to someone else's servers.

Because Dyad runs locally and lets you bring your own AI API keys, there is no vendor lock-in. It is a cross-platform desktop app that is easy to set up on Mac or Windows, and you can download and start using it with no sign-up required.
features: Runs locally on your machine for speed and privacy, with no cloud dependency
Bring your own AI API keys, so you avoid vendor lock-in
Cross-platform desktop app that works on Mac and Windows
No sign-up required to download and start building
Open-source code (Apache 2.0) that you can read, run, and contribute to

gettingStarted.intro: The fastest way to use Dyad is to download the ready-made desktop app. No sign-up is needed. Developers who want to work on the code can also run it from source as an Electron app.

steps.heading: Download the app
steps.body: Go to the Dyad download page and grab the build for your platform (Mac or Windows). No account or sign-up is required, so you can install it and start building right away.

steps.heading: Build from source: install dependencies
steps.body: If you want to run Dyad from source, clone the repository and install the Node dependencies. Dyad is an Electron app.

steps.heading: Create the userData directory
steps.body: Dyad needs a userData directory for its local database. Create it before the first run.

steps.heading: Run Dyad locally
steps.body: Start the app in development mode. This launches Dyad on your machine so you can build and test AI apps locally.

useCases: Building AI-powered apps locally without sending your code and data to a cloud service
Prototyping apps quickly with your own AI provider keys instead of a locked-in platform
Working privately on Mac or Windows with full control over your project and tooling
Contributing to or self-hosting an open-source alternative to cloud app builders like Lovable, v0, or Bolt
